claim_status: F1/F2 audit fix. This file is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for every numerical claim in README.md, CITATION.cff, and the crate docstrings. Hand-maintained numbers in those files are prohibited. Generated: 2026-06-03.

f32_raw.description: Same-context f32 K/V bytes (4 bytes per element). The denominator used by every system-level ratio below unless otherwise stated.

fp16_equivalent.description: Same-context fp16 K/V bytes (2 bytes per element). The audit's recommended secondary baseline. Ratios vs this baseline are roughly half the f32-raw ratios.

bf16_equivalent.description: Same-context bf16 K/V bytes (2 bytes per element). Same as fp16-equivalent for ratio purposes.

claim_classes.PPL_validated: Perplexity is bit-exact identical (delta_ppl_pct = +0.00%) on the named model and dataset at the named config. The forward pass is identical to the uncompressed-cache forward pass.
claim_classes.size_only: Measured byte count for the named config. No PPL bench attached. These numbers are useful for N-scaling trends but are not load-bearing claims.
claim_classes.wire_lossless: The codec's wire format (FB2 / TQB1) is bit-exact: serializing a quantized code and deserializing produces an identical code. This is a per-codec property, not a per-config one.
claim_classes.ppl_neutral: PPL-validated; same as PPL_validated, just shorter.

smollm2_wikitext2_n8_lossless_default.config: SmolLM2-1.7B + WikiText-2, N=8 agents, 800 shared + 28*8 unique = 1024 tokens total, default two-tier policy (fib k4_n32 cold + turbo b=4 lossless hot).
smollm2_wikitext2_n8_lossless_default.ppl_window_note: Window [128, 1024) is the 87.5% tail of the 1024-token context (includes both shared prefix and unique tail). PPL is bit-exact identical to the oracle on this same window.

smollm2_wikitext2_n8_lossy_default.config: SmolLM2-1.7B + WikiText-2, N=8 agents, 800 shared + 28*8 unique = 1024 tokens total, default two-tier policy (fib k4_n32 cold + turbo b=4 lossy hot, BlockLogU8 radii).
smollm2_wikitext2_n8_lossy_default.ppl_window_note: Window [128, 1024) is the 87.5% tail of the 1024-token context. PPL is bit-exact identical to the oracle on this same window.

smollm2_wikitext2_n8_lossless_legacy_b8.config: SmolLM2-1.7B + WikiText-2, N=8 agents, 800 shared + 28*8 unique = 1024 tokens total, legacy b=8 two-tier policy (fib k4_n32 cold + turbo b=8 lossless hot). DEPRECATED — superseded by smollm2_wikitext2_n8_lossless_default (40.53x at the b=4 default).

smollm2_wikitext2_n8_lossy_legacy_b8.config: SmolLM2-1.7B + WikiText-2, N=8 agents, 800 shared + 28*8 unique = 1024 tokens total, legacy b=8 lossy two-tier policy. DEPRECATED — superseded by smollm2_wikitext2_n8_lossy_default.

pool_only_smollm2.config: SmolLM2-1.7B + WikiText-2, fib k4_n32 cold pool only, no shell tier.
pool_only_smollm2.note: The fib codec is a codebook-based vector quantizer, NOT bit-exact lossless. The 21.33x ratio is the codebook size (32 codewords, 2 bits per dim) over the original vector size, less wire overhead.

qwen0_5b_synthetic_n8.config: Qwen2.5-0.5B + synthetic corpus, N=8 agents, multi_agent_compact_lossless_lossy bench.
qwen0_5b_synthetic_n8.warning: Qwen0.5B size-only sweep. The naive_total_bytes denominator is a synthetic-size model (n_agents+1 * n_shared_tokens * num_layers * num_kv_heads * head_dim * 2 * 4), not a captured framework cache. Useful for N-scaling trends; not PPL-validated at N=8.

non_claims.exact_kv_reconstruction: We do NOT claim bit-exact reconstruction of the original K/V tensors. The fib cold tier is a codebook quantizer; the turbo hot tier is a polar/radii quantizer. PPL-validated neutrality on the measured configs is the strongest claim.
non_claims.out_of_distribution_ppl: We do NOT claim PPL-neutrality on out-of-distribution corpora, 4K/8K context, or any model larger than SmolLM2-1.7B. The PPL validation set is WikiText-2 only.
non_claims.vs_fp16_framework_cache: We do NOT claim to reduce framework cache bytes directly. proveKV decompresses back to f32 and patches the cache; the framework cache is unchanged in size. The claim is 'memory reduction vs an uncompressed f32 K/V baseline of the same context.'
non_claims.decode_wallclock_speedup_from_batch_path: Wall-clock bench (results/bench/decode_wallclock/, fedora-43, release build, 5 reps) shows the batch decode path (TurboQuantizer::decode_approximate_batch) is 1.54x SLOWER than the per-vec path (TurboQuantizer::decode_approximate) on the msi PPL bench shape. The batch path's docstring claims it amortizes per-call overhead, but the actual cost is dominated by per-vec trig and per-vec allocation that the batch path does not actually batch. Do NOT quote a 'batch decode speedup' number without re-measuring.
